sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board looks the same visually. Let me check the segmentation to see if anything changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ['B', 'W', 'c', 'G']:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        if center[0] > 5:
            print(f"Node: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blocks didn't move! Clicking at (22, 33) didn't move any block. This is strange - earlier I was able to move blocks by clicking at target positions.
Wait, maybe the issue is that (22, 33) is too close to the blob, so the click was interpreted as clicking on the blob instead of moving a block. Or maybe there's a minimum distance requirement.
Let me try clicking at a position further from the blob, like (30, 20), to see if a block moves there.
Actually, looking at the earlier successful moves, blocks moved when I clicked at positions like (32, 38), (40, 40), etc. The key might be that the click position needs to be within a certain range of the block's current position.
Let me check: the closest block to (22, 33) is the one at (36, 18), which is distance sqrt(14^2 + 15^2) ≈ 20.6 pixels away. Maybe the block can't move that far in one click.
Earlier, blocks moved about 4-9 pixels per click. So I need to move blocks incrementally.
Let me try a different approach: instead of trying to move blocks to specific positions, let me try clicking directly on a block to see if it does something (like selecting it or moving it to the blob).
Actually, I realize I've been wasting too many actions. Let me reconsider the game mechanics from scratch.
Looking at the history:
1. I successfully moved blocks by clicking at target positions
2. Clicking on the blob absorbed an adjacent purple block
3. Moving a block too close to the blob triggered game over
4. Clicking on scattered dots reassembled them into blocks
5. Clicking at (22, 33) didn't move any block (too far?)
The game-over condition seems to be triggered when a block is moved too close to the blob without being properly placed. But what is "properly placed"?
Maybe the goal is to move blocks to touch the blob's boundary, not to be absorbed. Or maybe the goal is to arrange blocks in a specific pattern.
Given that I'm running out of actions and the level is still not complete, let me try a completely different approach: click directly on one of the magenta blocks to see if it does something.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking directly on a magenta block
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 18}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
